rationale: Probes via rdm story mutation-probe, all KILLED. Clauses: (1) each verdict records its hash scope, module by default: faithfulness `DEFAULT_SCOPE = SCOPE_MODULE` -> `SCOPE_FUNCTION` KILLED (hash_scope == 'module' in the JSON record + the helper edit must stale the default pin); (2) module scope pins the FULL source files containing the verifying tests: module hashing `texts.append(Path(path).read_text(...))` -> `texts.append("")` KILLED (a helper-only edit must change the module hash); (3) function scope selectable and (4) staleness judged per verdict at its recorded scope: reconcile comparing function-scoped verdicts against the module hash (expected-map `SCOPE_FUNCTION: current_hashes(..., SCOPE_FUNCTION)` -> computed at SCOPE_MODULE) KILLED, and the recorder pinning at module regardless of the selected scope (design_gate record_verdict `scope=hash_scope` -> `scope=faithfulness.SCOPE_MODULE`) KILLED - together these prove a hash_scope='function' verdict is pinned AND judged at function scope; (5) legacy verdicts (no hash_scope field) honored as function-scoped: fold fallback `else SCOPE_FUNCTION` -> `else SCOPE_MODULE` KILLED (the hand-written legacy record must stay FAITHFUL). Documented surviving/equivalent mutants: the _status-branch legacy fallback mutant survives as dead code (_fold normalizes the scope first - equivalent); `if scope == SCOPE_MODULE:` -> `if True:` survives because the test computes the legacy hash with the same current_hashes call (tautological w.r.t. hashing internals) and because the second _edit_helper() is a no-op (the first edit already rewrote its search string), so the function-pin-survives-helper-edit intent is pinned only via the scope-consistency kills above - the DI's declared clauses are all covered.

probes.find: agg.hash_scope = v.hash_scope if v.hash_scope in _SCOPES else SCOPE_FUNCTION
probes.replace: agg.hash_scope = v.hash_scope if v.hash_scope in _SCOPES else SCOPE_MODULE
